skill.name: notebooklm
skill.description: Query Google NotebookLM notebooks directly from Claude Code for source-grounded, citation-backed answers powered by Gemini.
skill.summary: Browser automation skill that queries NotebookLM for document-based research with automatic follow-ups and citation tracking.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static scanner detected 558 pattern matches across 20 files, but evaluation confirms most are false positives in documentation. Real risks: browser automation saves Google auth cookies locally, network access to notebooklm.google.com, and subprocess execution for internal scripts. All patterns are expected for legitimate NotebookLM integration. Markdown files triggered external_commands detections from code examples. Recommend warnings about authentication requirements and network dependency.

medium_findings.title: Browser Credential Storage
medium_findings.description: Skill saves Google authentication cookies to local storage at ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/data/. This is necessary for browser automation but creates a local credential store.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of context.storage_state() saving cookies to disk - standard browser automation pattern but requires user awareness

medium_findings.title: External Network Dependency
medium_findings.description: Skill requires network access to notebooklm.google.com for all operations. Cannot function offline or if Google blocks automated access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct page.goto() calls to notebooklm.google.com - core functionality depends on external service availability

low_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for Script Runner
low_findings.description: run.py uses subprocess.run to execute Python scripts within the virtual environment. All commands are hardcoded with no user input injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run with hardcoded script paths - legitimate internal process execution, not user-controlled

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Uses python-dotenv for optional .env configuration. Standard pattern for local configuration management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: python-dotenv is standard configuration library - low risk when not combined with exfiltration vectors

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory File Access
low_findings.description: Skill reads/writes to ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/data/ for local storage. This is the skill's designated data directory, not unauthorized access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Directory creation in skill's own data path - expected behavior, not suspicious hidden file access

content.user_title: Query NotebookLM Notebooks from Claude Code
content.value_statement: Stop the copy-paste dance between your browser and editor. This skill lets Claude Code chat directly with Google NotebookLM for source-grounded answers that cite your uploaded documents.
actual_capabilities: Authenticate with Google NotebookLM using persistent browser sessions
Query NotebookLM notebooks with automatic follow-up questions for comprehensive answers
Manage a local library of saved notebooks with metadata and tags
Retrieve citation-backed answers that reference specific sources in your documents
Automatically discover notebook content when adding new notebooks to library
Clean up browser state and cached data while preserving notebook library
limitations: Requires local Claude Code installation - does not work in web UI due to sandbox restrictions
Each question opens a fresh browser session - no persistent chat context between queries
Depends on Google NotebookLM service availability and your account rate limits (50 queries/day on free tier)
Requires manual document upload to NotebookLM - skill cannot upload documents automatically

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation Research
use_cases.description: Query API documentation, workshop manuals, or technical specs uploaded to NotebookLM. Claude asks comprehensive follow-ups to get complete implementation details, then writes correct code without hallucinating APIs.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working with complex documentation

use_cases.title: Academic Literature Review
use_cases.description: Upload research papers to NotebookLM and query them through Claude Code. Get synthesized answers with citations across multiple papers, enabling faster literature reviews and accurate referencing.

use_cases.title: Personal Knowledge Base Queries
use_cases.description: Build a library of notebooks containing your notes, meeting transcripts, and project docs. Query your entire knowledge base naturally through Claude, getting answers grounded in your actual content.
use_cases.target_user: Knowledge workers managing large document collections

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Simple Question
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask my NotebookLM: What are the key specifications for [topic] in my uploaded documents?
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick factual lookup from your documents

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Add Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Add this NotebookLM to my library: [notebook-url]. Query it first to discover what it contains, then save it with an appropriate name and topics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new notebook with automatic content discovery

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Multi-Aspect Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Query my [notebook-name] notebook about [topic]. Ask comprehensive follow-up questions until you have complete information about: (1) core concepts, (2) implementation details, (3) edge cases, and (4) best practices. Then synthesize all answers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep research requiring multiple query rounds

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Cross-Notebook Synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to understand [topic] across my entire notebook library. Query each relevant notebook about this topic, compare the perspectives, identify any contradictions or gaps, and provide a unified synthesis with source citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex research spanning multiple document sources

output_examples.input: What does my React documentation say about useEffect cleanup functions?
output_examples.output: According to your React docs (Chapter 3: Effects), cleanup functions in useEffect run before the component unmounts and before each re-render. Key points: (1) Return a cleanup function from useEffect, (2) Use cleanup to cancel subscriptions, clear timers, or abort fetch requests, (3) Cleanup prevents memory leaks. Example pattern shown in docs: useEffect(() => { const sub = subscribe(); return () => sub.unsubscribe(); }, []). Source: React-d guidebook, page 47.

output_examples.input: Add this notebook to my library: https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/xyz123
output_examples.output: I queried the notebook and discovered it contains: Python machine learning tutorials covering scikit-learn, pandas, and model evaluation. Added to your library as 'ML Fundamentals Guide' with topics: python, machine-learning, scikit-learn, pandas. You can now query it with commands like 'Ask my ML notebook about cross-validation techniques'.

best_practices: Always use the run.py wrapper script - it handles virtual environment setup automatically and ensures dependencies are installed correctly
Ask comprehensive questions with full context since each query opens a fresh browser session without memory of previous questions
Review NotebookLM answers critically and ask follow-up questions when the 'Is that ALL you need to know?' prompt appears - this ensures complete information gathering
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to use this skill in Claude Code web UI - it requires network access that the sandboxed environment does not provide
Do not expect persistent session memory between queries - each question is independent and needs full context included
Do not rely on this skill for time-sensitive or frequently changing information - NotebookLM only knows what you have uploaded to it

faq.question: Why doesn't this work in the Claude web interface?
faq.answer: The web UI runs skills in a sandbox without network access. Browser automation requires network connectivity to reach NotebookLM's servers. You must use local Claude Code installation.

faq.question: Is my Google account secure when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Authentication cookies are stored locally in ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/data/ on your machine. Your credentials never leave your computer. Consider using a dedicated Google account for automation if concerned.

faq.question: How is this different from the NotebookLM MCP server?
faq.answer: This skill runs directly in Claude Code without needing separate MCP configuration. The MCP server offers persistent sessions and works with more tools (Codex, Cursor), while this skill provides simpler setup for Claude Code users.

faq.question: What happens if Google detects automation and blocks access?
faq.answer: The skill includes humanization features like realistic typing speeds and natural delays. If blocked, clear browser data and re-authenticate. Using a dedicated Google account is recommended for automation.

faq.question: Can I use multiple Google accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need to clear authentication and re-login to switch accounts. Run 'Clear NotebookLM authentication' then set up again with your desired account.

faq.question: What if the skill stops working after a NotebookLM update?
faq.answer: Google may update NotebookLM's interface, breaking selectors. Check for skill updates first. If none available, you may need to wait for the skill author to update selectors for the new interface.
